<!-- source-hash: cf5240623c0f4394d3ce1adde57eb4cb -->
Maps internal audit log domain objects to their external REST API response DTOs, serving as the translation layer between core audit data models and the OpenFrame API surface.

## Key Components

| Method | Input | Output | Description |
|--------|-------|--------|-------------|
| `toLogResponse` | `LogEvent` | `LogResponse` | Maps a single audit log event to its REST response DTO |
| `toLogsResponse` | `GenericQueryResult<LogEvent>` | `LogsResponse` | Maps a paginated list of log events, preserving page metadata |
| `toLogFilterResponse` | `LogFilters` | `LogFilterResponse` | Maps available filter options including organizations, tool types, event types, and severities |
| `toLogDetailsResponse` | `LogDetails` | `LogDetailsResponse` | Maps a detailed log entry (includes full `content`/details field) to its response DTO |

## Usage Example

```java
@RestController
@RequiredArgsConstructor
public class LogController {

    private final LogService logService;
    private final LogMapper logMapper;

    @GetMapping("/logs/{id}")
    public LogDetailsResponse getLogDetails(@PathVariable String id) {
        LogDetails details = logService.findById(id);
        return logMapper.toLogDetailsResponse(details);
    }

    @GetMapping("/logs")
    public LogsResponse getLogs(LogQuery query) {
        GenericQueryResult<LogEvent> result = logService.query(query);
        return logMapper.toLogsResponse(result); // preserves pageInfo
    }

    @GetMapping("/logs/filters")
    public LogFilterResponse getFilters() {
        LogFilters filters = logService.getAvailableFilters();
        return logMapper.toLogFilterResponse(filters);
    }
}
```

## Notes

- All methods handle `null` inputs gracefully — returning `null` or an empty response rather than throwing.
- `toLogsResponse` returns an empty `logs` list (not `null`) when the result is `null`, safe for client consumption.
- Extends `BaseRestMapper`, inheriting any shared REST mapping utilities from the OpenFrame mapper hierarchy.
